messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent. Generate a small ordered sequence of Lean declarations for the provided LaTeX theorem-like unit. Use only the source text, selector plan, previous-project context if shown, and Lean-checked Mathlib signatures in the prompt. The original aligned Lean declarations, names, statements, and proofs are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "benchmark_policy": {
    "gold_comparison_is_posthoc_only": true,
    "posthoc_alignment_hidden": true,
    "target_lean_available_to_generator": false
  },
  "instructions": [
    "Do not use sorry, admit, placeholders, or comments standing in for proof.",
    "Do not include import statements or markdown fences.",
    "Do not ask for or infer the hidden aligned Lean declaration names/statements/proofs.",
    "Treat selector_unchecked_statement_sketch as non-authoritative mathematical intent only; do not copy its Lean syntax verbatim.",
    "Follow the Lean-checked signatures exactly when they differ from the selector's expected shape.",
    "Use the actual Lean-checked argument order from hydrated_mathlib_context. If the selector expected shape conflicts with the checked signature, the checked signature wins.",
    "When hydrated_mathlib_context includes a source_declaration or related_mathlib_declarations for a selected type/class, use those checked fields, projections, constructors, and extensionality facts instead of inventing absent APIs.",
    "Never use a hydrated Mathlib exact_identifier whose lean_check.status is not `checked`; treat it as unavailable even if the selector expected it to exist.",
    "Treat fallback_mathlib_candidates as search hints only. Do not cite or use a fallback candidate as a theorem unless its statement in the prompt is sufficient for the proof.",
    "If a selected Mathlib API is field-specific, unit-specific, or otherwise not strong enough for the source statement, say so in notes and generate only declarations justified by the visible context.",
    "Do not invent project helper names that are not shown in available_prior_project_context, needed_project_context, or source context.",
    "When available_prior_project_context contains Lean snippets for project definitions or predicates, use those exact names and statement shapes instead of rephrasing the source with raw hypotheses.",
    "When local_file_predecessor_declarations contains same-file helper declarations, you may reuse those exact helper names and statement shapes; do not infer any hidden target declaration from their file position.",
    "When planned_declarations contains role same_unit_helper, generate that helper declaration before any main_claim that lists it in depends_on_task_ids.",
    "Same-unit helper declarations are new declarations to create from the visible source unit. You may choose fresh descriptive names for them, but do not reuse or guess hidden aligned Lean names.",
    "Do not use a fresh helper name as a fact until the helper has already been defined earlier in lean_file_body.",
    "Prefer a narrow declaration sequence over a broad bundled conjunction when the source unit decomposes into multiple Lean declarations.",
    "When the source claim covers several cases such as upper/lower, left/right, or an `or` hypothesis, prefer separate narrow generated declarations for each case if the checked Mathlib APIs are case-specific. Avoid replacing case-specific source statements with a single disjunctive-hypothesis theorem unless the source explicitly requires that bundled statement.",
    "Keep theorem statements source-shaped. If a checked API uses an abstract predicate, structure, or library-specific hypothesis but the source states an elementary pointwise condition, keep the elementary source hypothesis in the generated theorem and construct the API hypothesis locally in the proof.",
    "When source terminology expands to multiple familiar cases, such as a property defined as either one case or another, cover the cases with separate source-shaped declarations when that is the natural Lean surface; do not replace the source theorem by one bundled disjunctive API-predicate hypothesis just to match a checked theorem.",
    "If a checked theorem expects a predicate such as `P x`, and the source hypothesis has exactly the pointwise content of that predicate, prove the predicate inline with `intro`/`exact`/`simpa` rather than exposing `P x` as a stronger replacement assumption.",
    "Keep theorem-local assumptions explicit rather than relying on unavailable global variables.",
    "If local_file_context_candidates show useful namespace, open, notation, or variable commands, you may include the needed commands in lean_file_body before the declarations.",
    "If you use a variable command instead of explicit binders, the command itself must appear in lean_file_body. Do not depend on file-scope variables that are not emitted in your output.",
    "Every identifier used in a theorem statement must be introduced by an explicit binder, local declaration, or imported/opened declaration. In particular, source parameters such as coefficient types, matrix sizes, rings, variables, and typeclass assumptions must be bound in the generated theorem, e.g. `{K : Type*} [CommRing K] {n : ℕ}` when the source quantifies over a commutative ring and a natural number.",
    "For finite sums and products over a Finset, use Lean's membership-binder notation `∑ x ∈ s, f x` and `∏ x ∈ s, f x`; Lean v4.28 does not accept `∑ x in s, ...` or `∏ x in s, ...`.",
    "When a checked bridge lemma rewrites between two indexed forms, use an explicit `rw [bridge_lemma]` or `change`/`rw` chain in the direction of the goal. Do not rely on `simpa [bridge_lemma] using source_theorem` unless the source theorem already has exactly the target indexed shape.",
    "If a proof combines a source theorem with a checked bridge rewrite, first rewrite with the source theorem when that exposes the bridge lemma's left-hand side, then apply the bridge rewrite; do not rewrite a bridge pattern before it occurs in the goal.",
    "If you cannot produce a complete proof from visible context, set status to cannot_prove_from_visible_context and leave lean_file_body empty. Never output Lean containing sorry/admit/placeholders.",
    "When status is cannot_prove_from_visible_context, do not use lean_file_body as a scratchpad. Put analysis in notes only; lean_file_body must be exactly \"\" and declaration_names exactly []."
  ],
  "lean_environment": {
    "default_opens_used_for_context_hydration": [
      "open scoped Polynomial BigOperators",
      "open PowerSeries Finset"
    ],
    "imports_available_in_verification_file": [
      "Mathlib"
    ],
    "toolchain": "leanprover/lean4:v4.28.0"
  },
  "required_json_schema": {
    "units": [
      {
        "declaration_names": [
          "names introduced in lean_file_body; empty list when status is cannot_prove_from_visible_context"
        ],
        "lean_file_body": "if status is generated: complete Lean declarations only, no imports or markdown; if status is cannot_prove_from_visible_context: exactly empty string",
        "notes": [
          "brief caveats or remaining uncertainty"
        ],
        "status": "generated|cannot_prove_from_visible_context",
        "unit_key": "unit-001",
        "used_context": [
          "source/project/Mathlib facts actually used"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "task": "Produce Lean declarations for each planned declaration task. The output should be a small Lean file body, without imports, containing the ordered declarations needed for this one source unit.",
  "units": [
    {
      "context_pack_size_risk": "medium",
      "formalization_risks": [
        "Transpose of partitions; extension to M-tuples; second Jacobi-Trudi formula using elementary symmetric functions"
      ],
      "local_file_context_candidates": [
        {
          "kind": "open",
          "line_range": [
            79,
            79
          ],
          "name": "open Finset BigOperators Matrix MvPolynomial",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
          "reason": "Local Lean context observed around safe prior project declarations. Use as placement/style guidance; do not assume hidden target declarations.",
          "source": "file_context_from_prior_project_declaration"
        },
        {
          "kind": "namespace",
          "line_range": [
            81,
            81
          ],
          "name": "SymmetricFunctions",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
          "reason": "Local Lean context observed around safe prior project declarations. Use as placement/style guidance; do not assume hidden target declarations.",
          "source": "file_context_from_prior_project_declaration"
        },
        {
          "kind": "variable",
          "line_range": [
            83,
            83
          ],
          "name": "variable {N : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
          "reason": "Local Lean context observed around safe prior project declarations. Use as placement/style guidance; do not assume hidden target declarations.",
          "source": "file_context_from_prior_project_declaration"
        }
      ],
      "planned_declarations": [
        {
          "available_prior_project_context": [
            {
              "project_declarations": [
                {
                  "context_source": "aligned_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [
                    "thm.sf.jt-h"
                  ],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.OmegaInvolution",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.LittlewoodRichardson"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "/-- First Jacobi-Trudi formula (Theorem thm.sf.jt-h)\n    Label: thm.sf.jt-h\n\n    s_{λ/μ} = det((h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j})_{1 ≤ i,j ≤ M})\n\n    The proof uses the Lindström-Gessel-Viennot lemma with appropriate\n    source and target vertices in the integer lattice ℤ².\n\n    ## Proof Strategy\n\n    **Step 1: Set up the LGV framework**\n    Define source vertices Aᵢ = (μᵢ - i, 1) and target vertices Bⱼ = (λⱼ - j, N) in ℤ².\n    The path weight matrix M_{i,j} = ∑_{p : Aᵢ → Bⱼ} w(p) where w(p) is the product\n    of x_h for each east-step at height h.\n\n    **Step 2: Identify M with the Jacobi-Trudi matrix**\n    By `pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm`, M_{i,j} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - j + i} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - (j - i)}.\n    Note: The textbook uses 1-indexed notation; our Lean formalization uses 0-indexed.\n    The matrix entry (i,j) is h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j} in the textbook convention.\n\n    **Step 3: Apply the LGV lemma**\n    By `lgv_nonpermutable` (Corollary cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np), since the source and\n    target vertices satisfy the sorting conditions (x-decreasing, y-increasing),\n    we have:\n      det(M) = ∑_{nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁} w(nipat)\n    where the sum is only over non-intersecting path tuples (nipats).\n\n    **Step 4: Establish the nipat-SSYT bijection**\n    By `nipat_ssyt_bijection`, there is a weight-preserving bijection between\n    nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁 and SSYT of skew shape λ/μ:\n    - The nipat (p₁, ..., pₖ) maps to the tableau T where row i contains\n      the heights of east-steps in path pᵢ (in weakly increasing order).\n    - Non-intersection of paths ensures column-strictness of the tableau.\n    - The weight w(nipat) = ∏ᵢ w(pᵢ) equals the monomial x^T.\n\n    **Step 5: Conclude**\n    det(M) = ∑_{nipats} w(nipat) = ∑_{SSYT T of shape λ/μ} x^T = s_{λ/μ}\n\n    ## Key Lemmas Required",
                  "line_range": [
                    8001,
                    8066
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.PieriJacobiTrudi",
                  "name": "SymmetricFunctions.jacobiTrudi_h",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np"
                  ],
                  "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
                  "snippet_truncated": true
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.OmegaInvolution",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.LittlewoodRichardson"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "theorem pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm (a c : ℤ) :\n    ∑ p ∈ latticePathFinset (N := N) a c, p.weight (R := R) = hsymmExt (N := N) (R := R) (c - a)",
                  "line_range": [
                    2846,
                    2868
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.PieriJacobiTrudi",
                  "name": "SymmetricFunctions.pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "thm.sf.jt-h",
                    "prop.lgv.1-paths.ct"
                  ],
                  "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                },
                {
                  "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
                  "declared_source_labels": [],
                  "imports": [
                    "Mathlib",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Determinants.LGV2",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.OmegaInvolution",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition",
                    "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.LittlewoodRichardson"
                  ],
                  "kind": "theorem",
                  "lean_snippet": "theorem nipat_ssyt_bijection (lam mu : Fin N → ℕ)\n    (hlam : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → lam j ≤ lam i)\n    (hmu : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → mu j ≤ mu i)\n    (hcontained : ∀ i, mu i ≤ lam i) :\n    ∃ f : Nipat lam mu hlam hmu hcontained →\n        SkewSSYT ⟨⟨lam, hlam⟩, ⟨mu, hmu⟩, fun i => hcontained i⟩,\n      Function.Bijective f ∧\n      ∀ np, np.weight (R := R) = (f np).toMonomial",
                  "line_range": [
                    3121,
                    3154
                  ],
                  "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.PieriJacobiTrudi",
                  "name": "SymmetricFunctions.nipat_ssyt_bijection",
                  "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
                  "referenced_source_labels": [
                    "thm.sf.jt-h"
                  ],
                  "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
                  "snippet_truncated": false
                }
              ],
              "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source unit with project declarations. These declarations are prior context, not aligned target declarations for the selected unit.",
              "source_labels": [
                "thm.sf.jt-h"
              ],
              "source_unit_id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.tex:thm.sf.jt-h"
            }
          ],
          "depends_on_task_ids": [],
          "hydrated_mathlib_context": [
            {
              "exact_identifier": null,
              "expected_signature_or_shape": "NPartition.transpose : NPartition N → NPartition (max part)",
              "fallback_mathlib_candidates": [],
              "fallback_suppression": null,
              "lean_check": {
                "error": "Exact identifier failed Lean check and was redacted from the generation prompt.",
                "status": "error"
              },
              "query": "<redacted failed exact identifier>",
              "related_mathlib_declarations": [],
              "request_index": 1,
              "source_declaration": null,
              "source_part": "whole unit",

              "unit_key": "unit-005",
              "usage_policy": "exact_identifier_failed_lean_check_redacted_do_not_use; fallback_mathlib_candidates are search hints, not checked facts",
              "why_needed": "transpose of a partition"
            },
            {
              "exact_identifier": "MvPolynomial.esymm",
              "expected_signature_or_shape": "esymm : Finset (Fin N) → MvPolynomial (Fin N) R",
              "fallback_mathlib_candidates": [],

              "lean_check": {
                "messages": [
                  {
                    "data": "MvPolynomial.esymm.{u_5, u_6} (σ : Type u_5) (R : Type u_6) [CommSemiring R] [Fintype σ] (n : ℕ) : MvPolynomial σ R",
                    "line": 12,
                    "severity": "information"
                  }
                ],
                "signature": "MvPolynomial.esymm.{u_5, u_6} (σ : Type u_5) (R : Type u_6) [CommSemiring R] [Fintype σ] (n : ℕ) : MvPolynomial σ R",
                "status": "checked"
              },
              "query": "MvPolynomial.esymm",
              "related_mathlib_declarations": [],
              "request_index": 2,
              "source_declaration": {
                "declaration_line": "def esymm (n : ℕ) : MvPolynomial σ R :=",
                "kind": "def",
                "line_number": 194,
                "name": "MvPolynomial.esymm",
                "path": ".lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/MvPolynomial/Symmetric/Defs.lean",
                "source_snippet": "def esymm (n : ℕ) : MvPolynomial σ R :=\n  ∑ t ∈ powersetCard n univ, ∏ i ∈ t, X i\n\n/--\n`esymmPart` is the product of the symmetric polynomials `esymm μᵢ`,\nwhere `μ = (μ₁, μ₂, ...)` is a partition.\n-/",
                "source_snippet_truncated": false
              },
              "source_part": "whole unit",
              "task_id": "unit-005-task-1",
              "unit_key": "unit-005",
              "usage_policy": "checked_signature_authoritative",
              "why_needed": "elementary symmetric functions"
            },
            {
              "exact_identifier": "Matrix.det",
              "expected_signature_or_shape": "Matrix.det of a matrix of MvPolynomial",
              "fallback_mathlib_candidates": [],
              "fallback_suppression": null,
              "lean_check": {
                "messages": [
                  {
                    "data": "Matrix.det.{v, u_2} {n : Type u_2} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] {R : Type v} [CommRing R] (M : Matrix n n R) : R",
                    "line": 4,

                  }
                ],

                "status": "checked"
              },
              "query": "Matrix.det",
              "related_mathlib_declarations": [],
              "request_index": 3,
              "source_declaration": {
                "declaration_line": "def det (M : Matrix n n R) : R :=",
                "kind": "def",
                "line_number": 59,
                "name": "Matrix.det",
                "path": ".lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/Determinant/Basic.lean",
                "source_snippet": "/-- The determinant of a matrix given by the Leibniz formula. -/\ndef det (M : Matrix n n R) : R :=\n  detRowAlternating M",
                "source_snippet_truncated": false
              },
              "source_part": "whole unit",
              "task_id": "unit-005-task-1",
              "unit_key": "unit-005",
              "usage_policy": "checked_signature_authoritative",
              "why_needed": "determinant of Jacobi-Trudi matrix"

          ],
          "kind": "theorem",
          "local_file_predecessor_declarations": [
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
              "kind": "structure",
              "lean_snippet": "structure NPartition (N : ℕ) where\n\n  parts : Fin N → ℕ\n\n  weaklyDecreasing : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → parts j ≤ parts i\n\nnamespace NPartition\n\nvariable {N : ℕ}",
              "line_range": [
                115,
                126
              ],
              "name": "NPartition",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
              "kind": "def",
              "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def schur (lam : NPartition N) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  ∑ T ∈ ssytFinset lam, T.toMonomial",
              "line_range": [
                1276,
                1285
              ],
              "name": "schur",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_dependency_of_local_predecessor",
              "kind": "def",
              "lean_snippet": "def verticalNStripPartitions (mu : NPartition N) (n : ℕ) : Finset (NPartition N) :=\n  (potentialVerticalStrips mu).filter (fun f =>\n    isWeaklyDecreasing f ∧ isVerticalStripFun f mu.parts ∧ hasSizeDiff mu.parts f n\n  ) |>.attach.image (fun ⟨f, hf⟩ =>\n    let hf' := Finset.mem_filter.mp hf\n    toNPartition f hf'.2.1)",
              "line_range": [
                1520,
                1531
              ],
              "name": "verticalNStripPartitions",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_structure_support",
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "@[ext]\ntheorem ext {lam mu : NPartition N} (h : lam.parts = mu.parts) : lam = mu := by\n  cases lam; cases mu; simp_all",
              "line_range": [
                126,
                130
              ],
              "name": "ext",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "theorem pieri_vertical (n : ℕ) (mu : NPartition N) :\n    esymm (Fin N) R n * schur mu =\n      ∑ lam ∈ verticalNStripPartitions mu n, schur lam := by\n  sorry",
              "line_range": [
                2631,
                2644
              ],
              "name": "pieri_vertical",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
              "kind": "def",
              "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def hsymmExt (n : ℤ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  if 0 ≤ n then hsymm (Fin N) R n.toNat else 0",
              "line_range": [
                2645,
                2648
              ],
              "name": "hsymmExt",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
              "kind": "theorem",
              "lean_snippet": "theorem hsymmExt_isSymmetric (n : ℤ) : (hsymmExt (N := N) (R := R) n).IsSymmetric := by\n  unfold hsymmExt\n  split_ifs with h\n  · exact hsymm_isSymmetric (Fin N) R n.toNat\n  · exact IsSymmetric.zero",
              "line_range": [
                2649,
                2656
              ],
              "name": "hsymmExt_isSymmetric",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
              "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
              "kind": "def",
              "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def jacobiTrudiMatrixH (lam mu : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    Matrix (Fin N) (Fin N) (MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) :=\n  Matrix.of fun i j =>\n    hsymmExt ((lam i : ℤ) - (mu j : ℤ) - (i.val : ℤ) + (j.val : ℤ))",
              "line_range": [
                2657,
                2661
              ],
              "name": "jacobiTrudiMatrixH",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            }
          ],
          "missing_or_uncertain_context": [

            {
              "name": "SymmetricFunctions.jacobiTrudi_h",
              "why_needed": "first Jacobi-Trudi formula; second formula can be derived via omega involution"

          ],
          "needed_source_context": [
            "Theorem thm.sf.jt-e: s_{λ/μ} = det((e_{λ_i^t - μ_j^t - i + j})_{1≤i,j≤M})"
          ],

            "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Pieri rules]\\label{thm.sf.pieri}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $\\mu$ be an\n$N$-partition. Then: \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} We have%\n\\[\nh_{n}s_{\\mu}=\\sum_{\\substack{\\lambda\\text{ is an }N\\text{-partition;}%\n\\\\\\lambda/\\mu\\text{ is a horizontal }n\\text{-strip}}}s_{\\lambda}.\n\\]\n\n\n\\textbf{(b)} We have%\n\\[\ne_{n}s_{\\mu}=\\sum_{\\substack{\\lambda\\text{ is an }N\\text{-partition;}%\n\\\\\\lambda/\\mu\\text{ is a vertical }n\\text{-strip}}}s_{\\lambda}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}"
          }

            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [

              "rmk.sf.N-par-vs-M-par"
            ],
            "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[First Jacobi--Trudi formula]\\label{thm.sf.jt-h}Let $M\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let\n$\\lambda=\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{M}\\right)  $ and\n$\\mu=\\left(  \\mu_{1},\\mu_{2},\\ldots,\\mu_{M}\\right)  $ be two $M$-partitions\n(i.e., weakly decreasing $M$-tuples of nonnegative integers). Then,%\n\\[\ns_{\\lambda/\\mu}=\\det\\left(  \\left(  h_{\\lambda_{i}-\\mu_{j}-i+j}\\right)\n_{1\\leq i\\leq M,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq M}\\right)  .\n\\]\n(Here, $s_{\\lambda/\\mu}$ is defined as in Definition \\ref{def.sf.skew-schur},\nwhere the semistandard tableaux of shape $\\lambda/\\mu$ are defined as certain\nfillings of $Y\\left(  \\lambda/\\mu\\right)  :=\\left\\{  \\left(  i,j\\right)\n\\ \\mid\\ i\\in\\left[  M\\right]  \\text{ and }j\\in\\left[  \\lambda_{i}\\right]\n\\setminus\\left[  \\mu_{i}\\right]  \\right\\}  $, but their entries are still\nsupposed to be elements of $\\left[  N\\right]  $. Compare with Remark\n\\ref{rmk.sf.N-par-vs-M-par}.)\n\\end{theorem}"
          }
        }

        "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Second Jacobi--Trudi formula]\\label{thm.sf.jt-e}Let $\\lambda$ and $\\mu$ be\ntwo partitions. Let $\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$ be the transposes of $\\lambda$\nand $\\mu$. Let $M\\in\\mathbb{N}$ be such that both $\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$\nhave length $\\leq M$. We extend the partitions $\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$ to\n$M$-tuples (by inserting zeroes at the end). Write these $M$-tuples\n$\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$ as $\\lambda^{t}=\\left(  \\lambda_{1}^{t}%\n,\\lambda_{2}^{t},\\ldots,\\lambda_{M}^{t}\\right)  $ and $\\mu=\\left(  \\mu_{1}%\n^{t},\\mu_{2}^{t},\\ldots,\\mu_{M}^{t}\\right)  $. Then,%\n\\[\ns_{\\lambda/\\mu}=\\det\\left(  \\left(  e_{\\lambda_{i}^{t}-\\mu_{j}^{t}%\n-i+j}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq M,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq M}\\right)  .\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}"
      },
